manifest.name: Flowtime

name: Flowtime

releases.author: Radialarray
releases.description:
## Fix

- **Add `display()` fallback** for Obsidian <1.13.0 (current stable)
  - Reads from `getSettingDefinitions()` — single source of truth for settings
  - Renders text/toggle/dropdown/number controls imperatively
  - Delegates custom renderers (templates, today note) to their render callbacks
- **Lower `minAppVersion`** back to 1.8.7

## How it works

| Obsidian version | Rendering path |
|---|---|
| <1.13.0 | `display()` → reads `getSettingDefinitions()` → imperative Setting API |
| ≥1.13.0 | Framework calls `getSettingDefinitions()` directly → declarative |

Both paths use the same definitions array — no duplicated settings code.
releases.name: 1.9.4 — Dual render path: display() + getSettingDefinitions()

releases.author: Radialarray
releases.description:
## Changes

- **Remove unnecessary type assertions** in main.ts (lines 95, 174, 176)
- **Fix unused variable warnings** — replace all `catch (_)` with `catch {}` (42 spots across 12 files)
- **Fix `_f` unused** — add `void _f` to mark destructured file property as intentionally evaluated
- **Refactor settings.ts** — remove bucket/sprint management UIs (now managed via Flowtime/ markdown files), convert `display()` to `getSettingDefinitions()`, override `getControlValue`/`setControlValue` for special settings

All Obsidian plugin review warnings should now be resolved.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Radialarray/flowtime-obsidian/compare/1.9.0...1.9.1
releases.name: 1.9.1 — Fix Obsidian review warnings

releases.author: Radialarray
releases.description:
## What's new in v1.7.0

58 commits since v1.3.0. Major themes: TypeScript migration, mobile markdown view, unified editor/timer, bucket definitions as markdown, and more.

### TypeScript Migration

- Full migration from JavaScript to TypeScript — all `src/*.js` → `src/*.ts`
- Strict null checks, typed interfaces across all modules
- Build pipeline hardened: `tsc --noEmit` + ESLint (zero warnings, fail on any warning)

### Buckets as Markdown (Canonical Source)

`Flowtime/Buckets.md` is now the canonical source for bucket definitions. Agents can read/write YAML frontmatter directly — no Obsidian API needed. The plugin syncs settings ↔ markdown file on every save.

### Auto-Process Inbox

`Cmd+P` → `Flowtime: Auto-Process Inbox`. Scans every inbox line, auto-converts items with `@date` tags into proper task lines, routes them to the right file (daily note, project file if project tag is present, or active file). Items without a date stay in inbox.

### Extract to New Note — Append to Existing Page

If the first selected line already contains a `[[wikilink]]` to an existing page, the remaining content is appended to that page instead of creating a new note. The selection collapses to just the `[[wikilink]]`.

### Mobile Markdown View + MarkdownAggregator

- Enhanced Markdown Notes (`type: flowtime-list` frontmatter) — drag handles, checkbox toggle, inline timer, heading drop zones
- Mobile markdown view — all 6 task views working on mobile
- MarkdownAggregator — aggregates tasks from source files and injects into mobile/notes view
- Pointer-event drag-drop with heading-drop re-aggregation

### Unified Floating Editor & Timer

- Single floating editor component shared between table and list views — edit task text, date, duration, bucket, milestones, project
- Unified timer component — shared DOM and logic across table and list views
- Source links open in new tab — close to return via Tab History

### Tab History

When closing a tab (Cmd+W) that was opened from a Flowtime link, the plugin navigates back to the previous tab instead of the next one in the tab bar. Enabled by default in Settings → Notifications.

### Table & List View Polish

- Global sync across tables — one view mode toggle applies to all tables
- Column sizing refinements — date column fixed width, actions column, task column dominates
- List view time inputs clickable, fixed-width cells — no layout shift
- Timer display no longer jumps on value change
- Group select matches toolbar button style
- Actions column compact with flex-wrap

### Performance

- TaskIndex cache eliminates repeated full vault scans on every render
- Batched DOM reads and O(1) row lookups for drag-drop
- Index-based sorting with `@i:number`, unified across list and table views

### Settings & Config

- Default routines folder moved from `Routines/` to `Flowtime/Routines/`
- `Flowtime/` directory at vault root for agent-accessible markdown data
- Sprint definitions deprecated — replaced by Milestones (`@ms:name` tag)


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Radialarray/flowtime-obsidian/compare/v1.3.0...1.7.0
releases.name: v1.7.0

releases.author: Radialarray
releases.description:
First stable release.

All core features:

- Interactive task tables with 8 code block views (today, overdue, week, project, budget, sessions, weekplan)
- Inline time editing, countdown timers, status bar timer
- Inbox capture + Process Inbox modal (GTD-style)
- Recurring routines (daily, weekly, monthly, custom schedules)
- Weekplan view with list and grid timeline modes
- Project detection via frontmatter, folder notes, or `@p:` tags
- Time budgets (buckets) with weekly limits
- Session history tracking and analytics
- Filter, sort, group, column visibility, bulk operations
- Cross-table auto-refresh

releases.author: Radialarray
releases.description:
New commands added:

- **Process Inbox** — walk-through modal for inbox items
- **Create Daily Dashboard** — creates Dashboard.md at vault root
- **Create Weekly Dashboard** — creates Dashboard Weekly.md at vault root
- **Start Timer** — modal-based timer with task name + duration
- **Stop Timer** — stops active status bar timer

Bugfix: ProcessInboxModal was imported but never registered as a command.
